INACTIVE Proposed site plan approval to permit an existing 640 sq.ft. (59.5 sm), "Penguin Pick-Up (PPU)" building located on the retail/commercial lands at 1900 Eglinton Avenue East. The PPU is designed as a convenience pick-up facility for customers to retrieve on-line purchases. PPU staff retrieve the parcel from the PPU building and bring the item to customers who can park in the PPU designated parking area. The PPU is self-sufficient without the need to connect to utility services, such as stormwater or sewage, to operate. Hydro connection is provided via an existing light standard.

Zoning By-law Amendment application for the lands at 1-70 Eglinton Square, 1431 & 1437 Victoria Park Ave, 14, 18, 22 and 26 Engelhart Cres within the Golden Mile Secondary Plan Area to permit a mixed-use development. The application proposes six (6) tall buildings with building heights that range from 20 to 50 storeys and a six-storey mid-rise building. A total gross floor area of 318,654 sq. m (315,845 sq. m for residential uses and 2,809 sq. m for retail uses), 3,618 residential units and an FSI of 4.75 times the area of the lands are proposed. The existing three-storey residential buildings at 14, 18, 22 and 26 Engelhart Cres would be retained. The application is related to Official Plan Amendment application 16 230579 ESC 35 OZ (under appeal).

This is the first residential application (Block 12) for Site Plan Control related to the redevelopment of the former Celestica lands, now known as Crosstown.  This application proposes the construction of three towers of 32, 34 and 39 storeys atop a podium ranging from 2 to 5 storeys, a portion of which is the retained facade of the Parkin Building.  Also proposed on the site is an 8 storey mid-rise, and 18 three storey back-to-back townhouses. Eight if the 18 townhouses are proposed to be affordable ownership units.  A POPS connection is proposed through the site, connecting further public Street C with Block 14B, which will be open space. An underground garage will accommodate 957 vehicle parking spaces and 7 care-shares spaces.  1,260 bicycle parking spaces are also proposed, as well as one bike share station.

The application proposes to increase the permitted height and density from the approved heights of 35 and 37-storeys as per By-law 1263-2022 (OLT), to 46 and 58-storeys This height increase would result in a total gross floor area of 73,305 m2, comprised of 71,992 m2 of residential GFA and 1,313 m2 of non-residential GFA at-grade. A total of 1,206 residential units are proposed, including 553 units in the west tower and 653 in the east tower. Vehicular and loading access to the development continues to be proposed from the future east-west private laneway on-site, connecting Henning Avenue and Duplex Avenue. Three (3) levels of underground parking are proposed, containing a total of 216 vehicular parking spaces. The revised buildings maintain the setbacks, stepbacks, commercial uses, and heritage retention of the previous approval.

Rezoning Application to redevelop the site with a 43-storey mixed-use building with a total net FSI of 24.77 and a lot coverage of approximately 71%.  The Proposal includes a 6-storey podium building, which would include retail and residential lobby on the Ground Floor, indoor amenity space on the 2nd Floor, indoor amenity space and residential units on the 3rd Floor and 3 floors of residential units, with the tower containing additional residential uses above.  In total, the Proposal will contain a GFA of approximately 29,622.7 sq. m. consisting of approximately 183.4 sq. m. of retail space and approximately 29,438.3.0 sq.m. of residential space. This provides for a proposed residential unit count of 442 apartment dwellings.

Site Plan application for the redevelopment of the existing 8.8 acre site with a total density of 3.87.  The proposal includes 5,230m² of retail, 5-storey office (12,274 m²), 109,608 of residential (1,616 units), a new public park (0.32 ha),  daycare space (1,224 m²) and a network of public and private streets.  The proposal includes 10, 10, 18 and 21-storey buildings along Laird Drive and Eglinton Avenue East and buildings at 29 and 34-storeys in height towards the east end of the site. Also associated with a Zoning By-law Amendment application file no. 18 201853 NNY 26 OZ and Draft plan of subdivision application file no. 18 201865 NNY 26 SB.

Rezoning for proposed 40-storey tower with an 8-storey podium and three-levels of underground parking. The proposal incorporates residential, retail, and office uses, and will replace the existing commercial/retail spaces. The building includes 479 units, 164 vehicular parking spaces, and 504 bicycle parking spaces.

Proposal for three buildings based on an approved Municipal Zoning Order. A total of 997 dwelling units, including 282 affordable rental dwelling units (approximately 28% of the total), a 955 square metre community space and 505 square metres of retail space are proposed. Building A (at the central northern part of the site) is a 34-storey tower with retail space on the ground floor and 314 dwelling units above. Building B (at the central southern part of the site) is a 31-storey tower with the existing machine shop building conserved and integrated into the base of the tower. Building B includes retail space and a community space on the ground floor and 282 affordable rental dwelling units above. Building C (at the northeastern corner of the site) is a 43-storey residential tower that includes 401 dwelling units. The existing cleaning room building at the northwestern corner of the site is proposed to be conserved, with the potential to include non-residential uses, but is not currently identified as part of the proposed development. The MZO is available at https://www.ontario.ca/laws/regulation/200595

Site Plan Control application to permit Phase 1 of the development at 1891 Eglinton Ave E, which will consist of two mixed-use buildings on the northwest portion of the lands. The western building (Building A) will have a height of 48 storeys (152.5 m. excluding mechanical penthouse), a total Gross Floor Area (GFA) of 36,824 sq. m. (34,170 sq. m. for residential uses, 1,172 sq. m. for retail uses and 1,482 sq. m. for office uses) and 568 residential units. The eastern building (Building B) will have a height of 44 storeys (141.0 m. excluding mechanical penthouse), a total GFA of 31,668 sq. m. (30,577 sq. m. for residential uses, 531 sq. m. for retail uses and and 560 sq. m. for child care uses) and 512 residential units. The buildings will be connected by a base building that ranges in height from two (2) to six (6) storeys. The application proposes a total of 458 parking spaces on three (3) levels below ground, 777 bicycle parking spaces and three (3) loading spaces. This application is related to Official Plan Amendment Application No. 20 158264 ESC 20 OZ (under appeal) and Zoning By-law Amendment Application No. 20 224755 ESC 20 OZ (under appeal).

Rezoning application to permit a mixed-use development on the northerly portion of the site. City Initiated Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment seeking to permit two buildings on site. Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit the redevelopment of the site with a 40-storey mixed use building that would include 387 residential units, two levels of underground parking with 187 vehicle parking spaces, 699 square metres of retail space on the ground floor and 2,169 square metres of office space. This is the site plan control application for Phase 1 of the lands. Site Plan Control application to permit the development of Phase One and Phase Two of the redevelopment at 250 Wincott Drive and 4620 Eglinton Avenue West. The proposed development includes Building C, a new 12-storey (49 metres, including the mechanical penthouse), and Building D, an existing one-storey commercial building proposed to be partially demolished, altered (enhanced with a series of brick, metal and glass materials), and will continue to be used for retail commercial purposes.

Site Plan application for the approval of a 48-storey non-residential building (218.55 metres), inclusive of a 6-storey podium, having a gross floor area of 114,586 square metres. A total of 368 vehicle parking spaces and a total of 542 bicycle parking spaces are proposed.  Vehicle access is proposed via a private driveway with access on the new Broadview Avenue and New Street E.  Access to the underground parking is proposed from New Street E.  The application includes a proposed 1000 sqm Privately Owned Publically accessible Space (POPS) along Broadview Avenue.This is Building 1B located in the north west quadrant adjacent to the furutre East Harbour Transit Hub. The East Harbour site is the subject of a Ministerial Zoning Order permitting 1,228,000 sq m of development, including 926,000 sq m of commercial development and 302,000 sq m of residential and is also subject to site specific zoning  by-laws 1280-2018 and 1281-2018.  This application is related to the subdivision application 16 270078 STE 30 SB which is currently under review by staff.

Bousfields Inc. has submitted an application for Zoning By-law Amendment.  The application proposes three mixed-use buildings with heights of 25, 30 and 37 storeys. The proposed development would be deployed in 2 phases with phase 1 consisting of the 37-storey building and phase 2 consisting of the 25 and 30 storeys buildings. The application proposes 935 residential units with a mix of unit types resulting in a residential gross floor area of 67,992 square metres. The application also proposes 522 square metres of retail/commercial uses and an overall floor space index of 7.36 times. A total of 742 vehicular and 714 bicycle parking spaces and 3 type G and 6 type C loading spaces would be provided. The proposal would provide a new 930 square-metre public park at the western portion of the site, pedestrian connections and privately-owned publicly accessible open spaces. A related Subdivision Application No. 21 201457 ESC 21 SB to create 4 new blocks has also been submitted by the applicant.

The proposal is for the redevelopment of the existing 6.7 acre site with a total density of 3.8 times the site area. The proposed includes 3,633 m² of retail commercial space, a network of new public and private streets, two new privately-owned publicky accessible open spaces and a 3,378 m² off-site parkland dedication. The proposal includes 4 new residential buildings (containing 1,279 units) ranging in height from 16 to 28 storeys (57.5 to 93 metres, excluding mechanical penthouses).

The proposed development is an 11-storey mixed-use rental apartment building which will be marketed toward students with 456.73 square metres retail at grade facing Ellesmere Road. The proposed development will provide a total of 256 rental units, being a mix of 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, 3-bedroom and 4-bedroom units, a total GFA of 18,542.03 square metres and a FSI of 5.06. It also provides 51parking spaces and 209 bicycle parking spaces.
Draft Plan of Condo for 301 units.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of an 46-storey mixed-use building providing residential and commercial use. The building will provide a total of 636 residential units, being a mix of studio, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom units. The building will also provide retail at grade. A paved access from Eldora Ave along the north property line will be provided, leading to 3 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 154 parking spaces; as well as 370 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 39,191.50 square metres and a FSI of 15.27. Additionally, the proposed development provides 1,443.50 square metres of indoor amenity space and 469.70 square metres of outdoor amenity space.
Draft Plan of Condominium Application to create 80 new units.
The proposal involves a 1,988.02 square meters warehouse addition with loading docks to the existing 1,446.64 square meters building for a total of 3,434.66 square meters.

Official Plan Amendment and Zoning By-law Amendment Application proposes the development of a 35-storey mixed-use building. The building will provide a total of 383 residential units and 99 vehicular parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total gross floor area of 281,538 square feet.

Site Plan Control Application proposes the development of a 6-storey municipal shelter building containing a total of 36 sleeping rooms, comprising a mix of 1, 2, 3, and 4-bed rooms, for a total of 76 beds. A paved access for Wheel-Trans loading is proposed along Eglinton Avenue West, adjacent to the southern property line. No below-grade parking levels are proposed; however, 19 bicycle parking spaces will be provided on site. The proposed development has a total GFA of 2,438.57 square metres, resulting in an FSI of 2.68. In addition, the proposal includes 344.30 square metres of indoor amenity space and 240.70 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

Province submitted a Transit-Oriented Community proposal for Martin Grove (Martin Grove TOC).  Located at the north east corner of Martin Grove Road and Eglinton Ave. West adjacent to Eglinton Crosstown West Extension - Martin Grove Station.

The proposed development is Phase 1, Housing Now Program, and is comprised of two residential towers (Building A & B) atop a single podium. The ground floor of the podium contains non-residential uses. Floors 2, 3 and 4 of the podium primarily contain vehicle parking as well as mechanical and storage spaces. The application includes 612 residential units (306 non-profit market co-op units and 306 affordable co-op units).
